note: Codebase footprint section published in three places: index.html (// 00b CODEBASE FOOTPRINT, between Key Statistics and Overview), sost-protocol-spec.html (8b. Codebase Footprint, between section 8 Synchronization and section 9 System Requirements), and sost-whitepaper.html (// CODEBASE FOOTPRINT, between Key Specifications and the Decentralization Honest Assessment). All three carry the same table comparing each SOST component (blockchain core C++ 36 442 LOC, front-end + explorer 69 975 LOC, Materials Engine Python 161 282 LOC, Geaspirit 9 386 LOC, auth gateway 704 LOC, docs markdown 23 975 LOC, total code ~278k, total + docs ~302k) side by side with Bitcoin Core (~280–310k LOC). Same four-paragraph honest reading after each table: blockchain core alone is 5–7× smaller than Bitcoin Core; full SOST stack matches BTC only because it is a vertical including Materials Engine and Geaspirit; what the numbers do show (serious project with custom consensus and verticals); what the numbers do NOT show (network effect, audits, deployed value, decentralisation). Methodology footer: counts measured 2026-05-10 with find … | xargs wc -l, BTC figure from upstream bitcoin/bitcoin master snapshot. NO consensus, NO RPC schema, NO node, NO miner, NO transaction wire format. Pure documentation publish.
